name: Amazon DSP Programmatic Advertising API
what_it_does: Amazon's demand-side platform (DSP) with REST API — enabling programmatic advertising using Amazon's first-party shopper data, across Amazon-owned properties (Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch, Fire TV) and third-party open internet inventory, with unmatched purchase intent targeting for consumer brands selling on or off Amazon.
use_cases: Running programmatic display and video campaigns using Amazon shopper audience data
Retargeting Amazon product page visitors and shopping category browsers programmatically
Accessing Amazon Publisher Direct inventory (Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch) via DSP API
Pulling campaign performance data and sales attribution for Amazon DSP reporting
Managing audience segments and custom audience creation via Amazon DSP API
not_for: Amazon Sponsored Products/Brands/Display (use Amazon Advertising API for sponsored ads)
Non-Amazon retail media (use The Trade Desk or Criteo for non-Amazon retail media)
Small advertisers — Amazon DSP has $35K+ minimum spend requirements for self-service
best_when: A consumer brand selling on Amazon or reaching Amazon shopper audiences needs programmatic advertising with first-party Amazon purchase intent data — especially for CPG, consumer electronics, apparel, and home goods categories.
avoid_when: You don't sell on Amazon, target B2B audiences, need social or search advertising, or have programmatic budgets below $35K/month.

auth.notes: Amazon Advertising API uses Login with Amazon (LwA) OAuth2. Authorization code flow. Bearer token with 1-hour expiry and refresh token. Profile ID required for all API calls (advertiser profile). No webhooks — must poll for reporting. Async reporting model. SDK available for Java and Python. DSP API separate from Sponsored Ads API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; $35K+ minimum for self-service DSP
pricing.paid_tiers: Technology fee as percentage of managed spend; contact Amazon Advertising sales
pricing.notes: Amazon Advertising division. Amazon DSP part of Amazon Ads ecosystem. $40B+ Amazon Advertising revenue (2023). Amazon first-party shopper data is the key differentiator — 200M+ Prime members with purchase history. Amazon Publisher Direct for premium Amazon-owned inventory (Amazon.com, IMDb, Twitch). Connected TV strength via Fire TV and IMDb TV. Amazon Attribution for cross-channel sales measurement.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per profile; enterprise limits via Amazon Advertising API

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for campaigns, line items, audiences, and reporting. LwA OAuth2 auth. Good developer documentation. Separate DSP API from Sponsored Ads API — different endpoints and auth scopes. Profile ID required for all calls. Async reporting — generate, poll, download. SDK for Java and Python. Amazon Attribution for sales measurement.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Campaign reads fully idempotent. Audience reads idempotent. Report generation not idempotent — use report ID for status polling. Creative uploads not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: LwA OAuth2 access token expires in 1 hour — implement refresh token rotation
Profile ID required for all API calls — different profiles for different advertisers/regions
Async reporting — generate report, poll for completion, download when ready
DSP API and Sponsored Ads API are separate — different endpoints and scopes
Amazon shopper audience targeting has data clean room restrictions — cannot export raw audience data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Multi-region data residency. LwA OAuth2 with scopes. Amazon security standards (AWS-level). Amazon first-party audience data is highly valuable — no raw export allowed; data clean room model. GDPR consent management required for EU audience targeting. Amazon Attribution data shares sales data — handle as financial data.
